# Built-in Functions

> **Hands-on:** Try the [Perform Dynamic Operations with Functions](/terraform/tutorials/configuration-language/functions?utm_source=WEBSITE&utm_medium=WEB_IO&utm_offer=ARTICLE_PAGE&utm_content=DOCS) tutorial.

The Terraform language includes a number of built-in functions that you can
call from within expressions to transform and combine values. The general
syntax for function calls is a function name followed by comma-separated
arguments in parentheses:

```hcl
max(5, 12, 9)
```

For more details on syntax, see
[_Function Calls_](/terraform/language/expressions/function-calls)
in the Expressions section.

## Provider-defined Functions

You cannot define your own functions in the Terraform configuration language, but
you can develop your own providers that expose functions. Refer to
[Provider-defined functions](/terraform/plugin/framework/functions)
for information about defining functions in custom providers.

When using a provider-specific function, add the `provider::<local-name>::` where
`<local-name>` corresponds with an entry in the `required_providers` block.
The following example calls the `encode_tfvars` function in the `terraform` provider:

```hcl
provider::terraform::encode_tfvars({
  example = "Hello!"
})
```

The documentation covers the built-in functions and built-in provider-defined functions only.

Functions defined by external providers are documented by those providers
in the [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/browse/providers).

## Experimenting with Functions

You can experiment with the behavior of Terraform's built-in functions from
the Terraform expression console, by running
[the `terraform console` command](/terraform/cli/commands/console):

```
> max(5, 12, 9)
12
```

The examples in the documentation for each function use console output to
illustrate the result of calling the function with different parameters.

## Function support by configuration file type

Standard Terraform configuration files support all functions, but other
configuration types, such as Terraform Stacks, only support a subset of
Terraform functions in deployment and component configurations. The following
tables describe function support across Terraform configuration types.

### Numeric functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [ceil](/terraform/language/functions/ceil)         | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | Returns the closest whole number that is greater than or equal to the given value, which may be a fraction. |
| [floor](/terraform/language/functions/floor)       | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | Returns the closest whole number that is less than or equal to the given value, which may be a fraction. |
| [log](/terraform/language/functions/log)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | Returns the logarithm of a given number in a given base. |
| [max](/terraform/language/functions/max)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | Returns the greatest number from a set of one or more numbers. |
| [min](/terraform/language/functions/min)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | Returns the smallest number from a set of one or more numbers. |
| [parseint](/terraform/language/functions/parseint) | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | Parses a given string as a representation of an integer in the specified base and returns the resulting number. |
| [pow](/terraform/language/functions/pow)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | Calculates an exponent, by raising its first argument to the power of the second argument. |
| [signum](/terraform/language/functions/signum)     | .hcl | Determines the sign of a number, returning `-1`, `0`, or `-1` to represent the sign. |

### String functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [chomp](/terraform/language/functions/chomp)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | removes newline characters at the end of a string |
| [endswith](/terraform/language/functions/endswith)           | .hcl | returns true if the given string ends with the given suffix, false otherwise |
| [format](/terraform/language/functions/format)         | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | produces a string by formatting a number of other values according to a specification string |
| [formatlist](/terraform/language/functions/formatlist) | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | produces a list of strings by formatting a number of other values according to a specification string |
| [indent](/terraform/language/functions/indent)         | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | adds a specified number of spaces to the beginning of each line in a multi-line string, except for the first line |
| [join](/terraform/language/functions/join)             | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | produces a string by concatenating all of the elements of the specified list of strings with the specified separator |
| [lower](/terraform/language/functions/lower)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | converts all cased letters in the given string to lowercase |
| [regex](/terraform/language/functions/regex)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | applies a regular expression to a string and returns the matching substrings |
| [regexall](/terraform/language/functions/regexall)     | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | applies a regular expression to a string and returns a list of all matches |
| [replace](/terraform/language/functions/replace)     | .hcl | searches a given string for another given substring, and replaces each occurrence with a given replacement string |
| [split](/terraform/language/functions/split)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | produces a list by dividing a given string at all occurrences of a given separator |
| [startswith](/terraform/language/functions/startswith)           | .hcl | returns true if the given string begins with the given prefix |
| [strcontains](/terraform/language/functions/strcontains)           | .hcl | returns true if the first string contains the second |
| [strrev](/terraform/language/functions/strrev)         | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | reverses the characters in a string |
| [substr](/terraform/language/functions/substr)         | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | extracts a substring from a given string by offset and length |
| [templatestring](/terraform/language/functions/templatestring)         | .hcl | renders a string as a template using a set of variables |
| [title](/terraform/language/functions/title)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | converts the first letter of each word in the given string to uppercase |
| [trim](/terraform/language/functions/trim)             | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | removes the specified set of characters from the start and end of the given string |
| [trimprefix](/terraform/language/functions/trimprefix) | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | removes the specified prefix from the start of the given string |
| [trimsuffix](/terraform/language/functions/trimsuffix) | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | removes the specified suffix from the end of the given string |
| [trimspace](/terraform/language/functions/trimspace)   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | removes any space characters from the start and end of the given string |
| [upper](/terraform/language/functions/upper)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | converts all cased letters in the given string to uppercase |

### Collection functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [alltrue](/terraform/language/functions/alltrue)       | .hcl | returns true if all elements in the given collection are true or "true", or if the collection is empty |
| [anytrue](/terraform/language/functions/anytrue)       | .hcl | returns true if any element in the given collection is true or "true", or the collection is empty |
| [chunklist](/terraform/language/functions/chunklist)       | .hcl | splits a single list into fixed-size chunks, returning a list of lists |
| [coalesce](/terraform/language/functions/coalesce)       | .hcl | takes any number of arguments and returns the first one that isn't null or an empty string |
| [coalescelist](/terraform/language/functions/coalescelist)       | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes any number of list arguments and returns the first one that isn't empty |
| [compact](/terraform/language/functions/compact)                 | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes a list of strings and returns a new list with any null or empty string elements removed |
| [concat](/terraform/language/functions/concat)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes two or more lists and combines them into a single list |
| [contains](/terraform/language/functions/contains)               | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | determines whether the list, tuple, or set given in its first argument contains at least one element that is equal to the value in the second argument |
| [distinct](/terraform/language/functions/distinct)               | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes a list and returns a new list with any duplicate elements removed |
| [element](/terraform/language/functions/element)                 | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | retrieves a single element from a list |
| [flatten](/terraform/language/functions/flatten)                 | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes a list and replaces any elements that are lists with a flattened sequence of the list contents |
| [index](/terraform/language/functions/index)             | .hcl | finds the first element for a given value in a list |
| [keys](/terraform/language/functions/keys)                       | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes a map and returns a list containing the keys from that map |
| [length](/terraform/language/functions/length)                       | .hcl | returns the length of a given list, map, or string |
| [length](/terraform/language/functions/length)                       | .hcl | returns the length of a given list, map, or string |
| [list](/terraform/language/functions/list)                       | &nbsp; | deprecated after Terraform 0.12. Use [`tolist`](/terraform/language/functions/tolist) instead |
| [lookup](/terraform/language/functions/lookup)                       | .hcl | retrieves the value of a single element from a map, given its key |
| [map](/terraform/language/functions/map)                       | &nbsp; | deprecated after Terraform 0.12. Use [`tomap`](/terraform/language/functions/tomap) instead |
| [matchkeys](/terraform/language/functions/matchkeys)                     | .hcl | constructs a new list by taking a subset of elements from one list whose indexes match the corresponding indexes of values in another list |
| [merge](/terraform/language/functions/merge)                     | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes an arbitrary number of maps or objects, and returns a single map or object that contains a merged set of elements from all arguments |
| [one](/terraform/language/functions/one)                     | .hcl | returns the only element in the given list, set, or tuple, null if the given argument is empty, or raises an error if it has more than one value |
| [range](/terraform/language/functions/range)                     | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | generates a list of numbers using a start value, a limit value, and a step value |
| [reverse](/terraform/language/functions/reverse)                 | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes a sequence and produces a new sequence of the same length with all of the same elements as the given sequence but in reverse order |
| [setintersection](/terraform/language/functions/setintersection) | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes multiple sets and produces a single set containing only the elements that all of the given sets have in common |
| [setproduct](/terraform/language/functions/setproduct)           | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | finds all of the possible combinations of elements from all of the given sets by computing the Cartesian product |
| [setsubtract](/terraform/language/functions/setsubtract)         | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | returns a new set containing the elements from the first set that are not present in the second set |
| [setunion](/terraform/language/functions/setunion)               | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes multiple sets and produces a single set containing the elements from all of the given sets |
| [slice](/terraform/language/functions/slice)                     | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | extracts some consecutive elements from within a list |
| [sort](/terraform/language/functions/sort)                       | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes a list of strings and returns a new list with those strings sorted lexicographically |
| [sum](/terraform/language/functions/sum)                       | .hcl | takes a list or set of numbers and returns the sum of those numbers |
| [transpose](/terraform/language/functions/transpose)                       | .hcl | takes a map of lists of strings and swaps the keys and values to produce a new map of lists of strings |
| [values](/terraform/language/functions/values)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | takes a map and returns a list containing the values of the elements in that map |
| [zipmap](/terraform/language/functions/zipmap)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | constructs a map from a list of keys and a corresponding list of values |

### Encoding functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [base64decode](/terraform/language/functions/base64decode)                   | .hcl | takes a string containing a Base64 character sequence and returns the original string |
| [base64encode](/terraform/language/functions/base64encode)                   | .hcl | applies Base64 encoding to a string and returns the encoded string |
| [base64gzip](/terraform/language/functions/base64gzip)                   | .hcl | compresses an HCL string using gzip and then encodes the string using Base64 encoding |
| [csvdecode](/terraform/language/functions/csvdecode)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | decodes a string containing CSV-formatted data and produces a list of maps representing that data |
| [jsondecode](/terraform/language/functions/jsondecode)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | interprets a given string as JSON, returning a representation of the result of decoding that string |
| [jsonencode](/terraform/language/functions/jsonencode)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | encodes a given value to a string using JSON syntax |
| [textdecodebase64](/terraform/language/functions/textdecodebase64)                   | .hcl | decodes a string that was previously Base64-encoded, and then interprets the result as characters in a specified character encoding |
| [textencodebase64](/terraform/language/functions/textencodebase64)                   | .hcl | encodes the unicode characters in a given string using a specified character encoding, returning the result base64 encoded |
| [textencodebase64](/terraform/language/functions/textencodebase64)                   | .hcl | encodes the unicode characters in a given string using a specified character encoding, returning the result base64 encoded |
| [urlencode](/terraform/language/functions/urlencode)                   | .hcl | applies URL encoding to a given string |
| [yamldecode](/terraform/language/functions/yamldecode)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | parses a string as a subset of YAML, and produces a representation of its value |
| [yamlencode](/terraform/language/functions/yamlencode)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | encodes a given value to a string using YAML 1.2 block syntax |

### Filesystem functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [abspath](/terraform/language/functions/abspath)                   | .hcl | takes a string containing a filesystem path and converts it to an absolute path |
| [dirname](/terraform/language/functions/dirname)                   | .hcl | takes a string containing a filesystem path and removes the last portion from it |
| [pathexpand](/terraform/language/functions/pathexpand)                   | .hcl | takes a filesystem path that might begin with a `~` segment, and if so it replaces that segment with the current user's home directory path |
| [basename](/terraform/language/functions/basename)                   | .hcl | takes a string containing a filesystem path and removes all except the last portion from it |
| [file](/terraform/language/functions/file)                   | .hcl | reads the contents of a file at the given path and returns them as a string |
| [fileexists](/terraform/language/functions/fileexists)                   | .hcl | determines whether a file exists at a given path |
| [fileset](/terraform/language/functions/fileset)                   | .hcl | enumerates a set of regular file names given a path and pattern |
| [filebase64](/terraform/language/functions/filebase64)                   | .hcl | reads the contents of a file at the given path and returns them as a base64-encoded string |
| [templatefile](/terraform/language/functions/templatefile)                   | .hcl | reads the file at the given path and renders its content as a template using a supplied set of template variables |

### Date and time functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [formatdate](/terraform/language/functions/formatdate)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | converts a timestamp into a different time format |
| [plantimestamp](/terraform/language/functions/plantimestamp)                   | .hcl | returns a UTC timestamp string in RFC 3339 format at the time Terraform creates a plan |
| [timeadd](/terraform/language/functions/timeadd)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | adds a duration to a timestamp, returning a new timestamp |
| [timecmp](/terraform/language/functions/timecmp)                   | .hcl | compares two timestamps and returns a number that represents the ordering of the instants those timestamps represent |
| [timestamp](/terraform/language/functions/timestamp)                   | .hcl | returns a UTC timestamp string in RFC 3339 format at the current time |

### Hash and crypto functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [base64sha256](/terraform/language/functions/base64sha256)                   | .hcl | computes the SHA256 hash of a given string and encodes it with Base64 |
| [base64sha512](/terraform/language/functions/base64sha512)                   | .hcl | computes the SHA512 hash of a given string and encodes it with Base64 |
| [bcrypt](/terraform/language/functions/bcrypt)                   | .hcl | computes a hash of the given string using the Blowfish cipher, returning a string in the Modular Crypt Format |
| [filebase64sha256](/terraform/language/functions/filebase64sha256)                   | .hcl | is a variant of base64sha256 that hashes the contents of a given file rather than a literal string |
| [filebase64sha512](/terraform/language/functions/filebase64sha512)                   | .hcl | is a variant of base64sha512 that hashes the contents of a given file rather than a literal string |
| [filemd5](/terraform/language/functions/filemd5)                   | .hcl | is a variant of md5 that hashes the contents of a given file rather than a literal string |
| [filesha1](/terraform/language/functions/filesha1)                   | .hcl | is a variant of sha1 that hashes the contents of a given file rather than a literal string |
| [filesha256](/terraform/language/functions/filesha256)                   | .hcl | calculates the SHA-256 hash of a file's contents |
| [filesha512](/terraform/language/functions/filesha512)                   | .hcl | calculates the SHA-512 hash of a file's contents |
| [md5](/terraform/language/functions/md5)                   | .hcl | computes the MD5 hash of a given string and encodes it with hexadecimal digits |
| [rsadecrypt](/terraform/language/functions/rsadecrypt)                   | .hcl | decrypts an RSA-encrypted ciphertext, returning the corresponding cleartext |
| [sha1](/terraform/language/functions/sha1)                   | .hcl | computes the SHA1 hash of a given string and encodes it with hexadecimal digits |
| [sha256](/terraform/language/functions/sha256)                   | .hcl | computes the SHA256 hash of a given string and encodes it with hexadecimal digits |
| [sha512](/terraform/language/functions/sha512)                   | .hcl | computes the SHA512 hash of a given string and encodes it with hexadecimal digits |
| [uuid](/terraform/language/functions/uuid)                   | .hcl | generates UUID-format strings using random bytes |
| [uuidv5](/terraform/language/functions/uuidv5)                   | .hcl | generates a name-based UUID, as described in RFC 4122 section 4.3 |

### IP network functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [cidrhost](/terraform/language/functions/cidrhost)                   | .hcl | calculates a full host IP address for a given host number within a given IP network address prefix |
| [cidrnetmask](/terraform/language/functions/cidrnetmask)                   | .hcl | converts an IPv4 address prefix given in CIDR notation into a subnet mask address |
| [cidrsubnet](/terraform/language/functions/cidrsubnet)                   | .hcl | calculates a subnet address within given IP network address prefix |
| [cidrsubnets](/terraform/language/functions/cidrsubnets)                   | .hcl | calculates a sequence of consecutive IP address ranges within a particular CIDR prefix |

### Type conversion functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [can](/terraform/language/functions/can)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | evaluates the given expression and returns a boolean value indicating whether the expression produced a result without any errors |
| [ephemeralasnull](/terraform/language/functions/ephemeralasnull)                   | .hcl | accepts an ephemeral value and returns `null` |
| [issensitive](/terraform/language/functions/issensitive)                   | .hcl | takes any value and returns `true` if Terraform treats it as sensitive |
| [nonsensitive](/terraform/language/functions/nonsensitive)                   | .hcl | takes a sensitive value and returns a copy of that value with the sensitive marking removed, thereby exposing the sensitive value |
| [sensitive](/terraform/language/functions/sensitive)                   | .hcl | takes any value and returns a copy of it marked so that Terraform will treat it as sensitive |
| [tobool](/terraform/language/functions/tobool)                   | .hcl | converts its argument to a boolean value |
| [tolist](/terraform/language/functions/tolist)                   | .hcl | converts its argument to a list value |
| [tomap](/terraform/language/functions/tomap)                   | .hcl | converts its argument to a map value |
| [tonumber](/terraform/language/functions/tonumber)                   | .hcl | converts its argument to a number value |
| [toset](/terraform/language/functions/toset)                   | .hcl | converts its argument to a set value |
| [tostring](/terraform/language/functions/tostring)                   | .hcl | converts its argument to a string value |
| [try](/terraform/language/functions/try)                   | .hcl,<br/>.tfcomponent.hcl,<br/>.tfdeploy.hcl | evaluates all of its argument expressions in turn and returns the result of the first one that does not produce any errors |
| [type](/terraform/language/functions/type)                   | .hcl | returns the type of a given value |

### Terraform-specific functions

| Function | Configuration type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [provider::terraform::encode_tfvars](/terraform/language/functions/terraform-encode_tfvars)                   | .hcl | takes an object value and produces a string containing a description of that object |
| [provider::terraform::decode_tfvars](/terraform/language/functions/terraform-decode_tfvars)                   | .hcl | takes a string containing the content of a `.tfvars` file and returns an object describing the raw variable values it defines |
| [provider::terraform::encode_expr](/terraform/language/functions/terraform-encode_expr)                   | .hcl | takes any value and produces a string containing Terraform language expression syntax approximating that value |
| [type](/terraform/language/functions/type)                   | .hcl | returns the type of a given value |
